reasoning: While the Metaculus community estimate of 57% on May 1, 2025, suggests strong sentiment that a federal AI security bill (possibly targeting Chinese espionage of frontier models) could pass by the end of 2025, searches have mostly returned voluntary initiatives (like the Secure A.I. Act) or state-level bills (like California's SB 1047 or Illinois HB 3506c). Historically, passing mandatory regulations for the tech industry is difficult and slow in Congress. Under a Republican-led government in 2025, there may be pushback against sweeping regulations, although national security concerns regarding frontier AI models could drive bipartisan support to attach cybersecurity mandates to a must-pass bill like the NDAA. Given the strict resolution criteria—a federal law passed by Dec 31, 2025, that mandates *all* U.S. actors possessing powerful models to take security measures—my probability is somewhat lower than the community consensus at 44%, reflecting the lack of a prominent, explicitly mandatory bill currently advancing.
